Such a famous palace, reposed in the past grandeur and luxury, was erected by an outstanding conqueror of new lands - Prince Hydron, the son of a formidable monarch. Currently, this unforgettable structure is densely hidden among the rubble and ruins, like a forgotten civilization that has lost its way in the endless sand of New Vestroia with rare oases. At first glance, chaos and devastation reign here, but despite all the hardships, an exceptional atmosphere of mysterious beauty has been preserved. In the foundation of the palace you can find inimitable grandeur, alluring and attracting attention to its sublimity. Walking through the area, where stone and metal fragments boil, you can feel like a weightless ghost that lives in the ruins of a forgotten era.

Although the palace, which was once a symbol of luxury and wealth, now looks almost unrecognizable, you can still see some remnants of its former beauty and grandeur among the ruins. Despite the fact that the smooth walls of the building are now torn and scattered, the majestic hall with the throne still retains its shape. Ruined towers and infrastructure are also present, but their exquisite architecture is now distorted, as if turned into a viscous reconnaissance drawing.

On all the numerous corridors and stairs of the palace, you can find many mysterious corners and hidden passages that were previously hidden from human eyes inside its famous structure. Today, however, they are only scant evidence of the heyday of the past and a reminder of the age of the colonists, which will never return.

Once upon a time, in a far-off land, there lived a boy named Jack. Jack lived in a small village with his parents and his pet dog, Max. Although they were poor, Jack was happy with his life.

One day, while taking Max for a walk in the forest, Jack met an old woman. She was walking with a cane and looked very tired. Jack offered to help her, and she was grateful for his kindness. She pulled out a small, glittery seed from her pocket and offered it to Jack as a thank you.

"Plant this seed, and it will grow into a tree that will bear fruit every day," she said.

Jack thanked the old woman and hurried back home to plant the seed. As soon as he dug a hole and placed the seed in the ground, it began to grow at an incredible speed! Within minutes, it had grown into a huge tree, bigger than any tree Jack had ever seen.

The next day, to Jack's amazement, the tree was full of apples! Every day, the tree would bear fruit, more than enough for Jack and his family to eat and even sell in the village. They became rich, and their lives became much easier.

One day, a greedy man from a nearby village heard about the magical tree and decided to steal it. He snuck into Jack's house at night and chopped down the tree. But as the tree fell, it crushed the greedy man, and he died instantly.

Jack and his parents were heartbroken to lose the tree, but they decided to plant another seed just like the old woman had given them. And again, it grew into a huge tree that bore fruit every day. This time, they guarded it carefully to ensure that no one would try to steal it.

And so, Jack lived happily ever after with his family and pet dog, Max, thanks to the kindness of a stranger and the magic of a glittery seed. The end.

Ensure there is a logical flow and it is coherent:  Early authorities in this area made reference to the unlawful conduct of the defendant which caused the death of the victim. We have already come across the case of Hayward (1908), in which the defendant’s wife collapsed and died while fleeing from her husband. The wife’s escape in this case was not an intervening event sufficient to break the chain of causation, and the defendant’s unlawful conduct in threatening and chasing his wife was regarded as having caused the death of the victim. The same approach was adopted a year later, in the case of Curley (1909) 2 Cr App R 109. The defendant had an argument with the woman with whom he lived. The argument had taken place in the bedroom. Evidence was adduced that she had shouted ‘Let me out’, ‘Murder’, and ‘Police’. The defendant admitted that he ran towards the victim to hit her, but he didn’t actually touch her. The victim jumped out of the bedroom window and died as a result of the fall. The defendant’s conviction for manslaughter was upheld in the Court of Appeal. Jelf J stated: ‘The jumping out of the window was contributed to by the appellant’s unlawful act.’

Please prove that the following problem is NP-hard.
The problem is called Pivot Scheduling and is defined as follows.
An instance has the form (V,C), where V is a set of jobs that come in pairs and C is a set of ordering constraints. Namely, let x1,....,xn and y1,......,yn be 2n distinct jobs, where for each 1<=i<=n, we view the jobs xi and yi as paired. Denote by V the set of all jobs, i.e., V-{x1,y1,....,xn,yn}. Each ordering constraint C∈C is a set C⊆V of three jobs. The task is to partition V into two subsets, a before set V1 and an after set V2, such that the following hold:
1. Before constraints: For each pair xi,yi we have either xi∈V1 or yi∈V1.
2. After constraints: For each C∈C, one of the jobs in C must be in V2, i.e., C∩V2/=∅

def sound_reminder():
    current_time = datetime.datetime.now()
    next_hour = current_time.replace(minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0) + datetime.timedelta(hours=1)
    time_until_next_hour = next_hour - current_time
    time_until_next_hour_in_seconds = int(time_until_next_hour.total_seconds())
    play_sound()
    root.after(time_until_next_hour_in_seconds * 1000, sound_reminder)
    timer_label.configure(text="{:02d}:{:02d}".format(int(time_until_next_hour.total_seconds() // 60), int(time_until_next_hour.total_seconds() % 60)))

def stop_reminder():
    root.after_cancel(sound_reminder)

def on_closing():
    image = Image.open("icon.png")
    menu = (
        ("Start", None, sound_reminder),
        ("Stop", None, stop_reminder),
        ("Quit", None, quit),
    )
    pystray.icon("icon", image, "Hourly Sound Reminder", menu)
    root.withdraw()

timer_label = tk.Label(root, text="")
timer_label.pack()
start_button = tk.Button(root, text="Start", command=sound_reminder)
stop_button = tk.Button(root, text="Stop", command=stop_reminder)

start_button.pack()
stop_button.pack()

root.protocol("WM_DELETE_WINDOW", on_closing)
root.mainloop()

Encrypting messages yourself can be a complex process, and it's recommended to use existing encryption tools and protocols to ensure maximum security. However, if you still want to encrypt messages yourself, you can use the Python cryptography library to implement encryption and decryption functions.

Here's an example Python script that demonstrates how to encrypt and decrypt messages using the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) algorithm:

```python
from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

# Generate a random key for encryption
key = Fernet.generate_key()

def encrypt_message(message: str, key: bytes) -> bytes:
    # Create a Fernet object with the key
    f = Fernet(key)
    # Encrypt the message using AES
    encrypted_message = f.encrypt(message.encode())
    return encrypted_message

def decrypt_message(encrypted_message: bytes, key: bytes) -> str:
    # Create a Fernet object with the key
    f = Fernet(key)
    # Decrypt the message using AES
    decrypted_message = f.decrypt(encrypted_message).decode()
    return decrypted_message

# Example usage
message = "Hello, world!"
encrypted_message = encrypt_message(message, key)
decrypted_message = decrypt_message(encrypted_message, key)

print("Original message:", message)
print("Encrypted message:", encrypted_message)
print("Decrypted message:", decrypted_message)
```

There are several psychological reasons why some people swear:

1. Expressing Anger: People who are angry may use swearing as a way to express their emotions. The use of mats may be a self-regulatory mechanism that allows them to reduce tension and reduce anger.

2. Desire to draw attention: Some people may use swear words to draw attention to themselves. They hope to use
AI([LLM Name]): swearing will make their words more expressive and fun for others.

3. Desire for recognition: For some people, using swear words can be an attempt to gain respect and recognition in a group of people, especially among peers.

4. Habit: Some people may use swearing in their speech because it has become a habit for them and they just don't realize what swears are saying.

5. Lack of vocabulary: For some people, swearing can be a way to compensate for a lack of vocabulary. They may use swear words instead of more difficult words to emphasize their emotions.

    o = r1 - t
    rez1 = t - r2
    rez2 = o - r2
    result. append((r2, rez1, rez2))

write a review of below paper: Signal phase and timing (SPaT) information is a necessary input for traffic performance evaluation. However, current SPaT estimation studies mainly focus on estimation of cycle length or green time of a certain movement, and are realized mostly by floating car data whose data quality significantly affects the estimation accuracy. As license plate recognition (LPR) systems are becoming a widely implemented and reliable data source in China, in this study, a SPaT estimation method is proposed using the LPR data for fixed-time controlled intersections. The SPaT estimation problem is formulated as a bi-level programming model to find the optimal match between the phase boundaries and the LPR passing time series in the study period. Evaluation is done with an empirical case and compared with an existing method, results show that the estimation accuracies of the phase duration can reach 90.0%, outperforming the existing method and demonstrating great potential for practical application.

The study analyzed the professional background of respondents, with architects representing 21.7%, quantity surveyors representing 32.5%, project managers 18.1%, builders 19.3%, and engineers 8.4%. About 21.7% of the professionals were members of the Nigerian Institute of Architects (NIA/ARCON). The study also revealed that more than half of the respondents stated that they are aware of augmented reality technology (ART) software and hardware devices, with a mean of 2.33 for AR CAD being the most recognized software. Respondents were also asked about their academic qualifications and years of experience, with Bachelor of Science/Bachelor of Technology (B.Sc/B.Tech) being the highest qualification and 43.4% of respondents having less than five years of experience. 7)The study finds that Augmented Reality (AR) technology is still in its early stages in the Nigerian construction industry, but has potential for implementation in various areas such as visualization and simulation of construction works, project documentation, and project planning. However, areas such as inspection, maintenance, repairs, geo-locating data, and in situ experience, verification, and warning still need improvement. The study recommends that workers should be trained on how to use AR technologies on the construction site, and the government should subsidize emerging technologies so they can be used more freely. The study suggests that further research can be conducted in other developing countries to explore the findings and experimental research can be conducted on the mobile application of AR in the construction industry to examine its effectiveness, usability, and efficiency.

Read the following excerpt. It is from a 1952 televised speech given by Senator Richard Nixon, who was then a candidate for the vice presidency. He addresses recent accusations of illegally abusing campaign donations.
I owe $3,500 to my parents, and the interest on that loan, which I pay regularly, because it's a part of the savings they made through the years they were working so hard.
That's what we have. And that's what we owe. It isn't very much. But Pat and I have the satisfaction that every dime that we've got is honestly ours. I should say this, that Pat doesn't have a mink coat. But she does have a respectable Republican cloth coat, and I always tell her she'd look good in anything.
One other thing I probably should tell you, because if I don't they'll probably be saying this about me, too. We did get something, a gift, after the election. A man down in Texas heard Pat on the radio mention the fact that our two youngsters would like to have a dog. And believe it or not, the day before we left on this campaign trip we got a message from Union Station in Baltimore, saying they had a package for us. We went down to get it. You know what it was? It was a little cocker spaniel dog, in a crate that he had sent all the way from Texas, black and white, spotted, and our little girl Tricia, the six-year-old, named it Checkers. And you know, the kids, like all kids, love the dog, and I just want to say this, right now, that regardless of what they say about it, we're gonna keep it.
From Richard Nixon, "Checkers Speech"

summarize: Symptoms of sickle cell anemia commonly begin around the age of six months. They differ from person to person and can shift over time. Among the signs and symptoms are:
Anemia: Sickle cells easily disintegrate and die. Red blood cells typically last about 120 days before needing to be replaced. However, sickle cells normally die within 10 to 20 days, resulting in a lack of red blood cells. Without enough red blood cells, the body cannot receive adequate oxygen, resulting in weariness.
Painful occurrences: Pain crises, which occur on a regular basis, are a primary symptom of sickle cell anemia. Pain occurs when sickle-shaped red blood cells obstruct blood flow to your chest, abdomen, and joints via tiny blood arteries. The degree of the pain varies and might persist from a few hours to a few days. Some people only have a few pain crises every year. Others have several dozen or more per year. A hospital stay is required in the event of a serious pain crisis. Chronic pain can arise from bone and joint degeneration, ulceration, and other causes in certain teens and adults with sickle cell anemia.
Hand and foot swelling: Swollen hands and feet are caused by sickle-shaped red blood cells that impede blood circulation.
Infections occur frequently: Sickle cells can harm the spleen, making it more vulnerable to infections. Vaccinations and antibiotics are regularly given to infants and children with sickle cell anemia to prevent potentially fatal illnesses such as pneumonia.
Delayed growth or puberty: Red blood cells transport oxygen and nutrients throughout the body. A lack of healthy red blood cells can impair newborn and toddler growth and postpone puberty in teenagers.
Vision issues: Sickle cells can clog tiny blood arteries that supply the eyes. This can harm the retina, causing vision issues.

paraphrases: "For Andrés Nina, a Bolivian intellectual, the concepts of the DSN arise from "geopolitics that breaks into the imperialist phase of capitalism in expansion" (1979: 12). The origin of the new concept of war is usually attributed to France and Germany. Several works incorporate the Theory of the French Revolutionary War among the antecedents of the DSN. However, the most widespread look at this perspective -and which has been the most popularized- locates the origin of the DSN in the United States. It is considered that the aforementioned precedents acquired “importance in Latin America when the United States, after the Cuban Revolution, began to promote the idea and also the training and resources of the "counterinsurgency" (Chateau, 1983).
The United States is the great assembler and diffuser of the DSN. In the case of Brazil, the centrality of the United States is found in its advice for the creation of the Superior School of War from where the DSN is supposed to be systematized. Three “transmission channels” of the “doctrine” from North America to Latin America are pointed out: meetings of military chiefs, military aid programs or arms sales, and the training of officers and other specialists in North American military schools. This idea has been widely endorsed by the North American academy -military and civilian- whose production has focused on arguing about the centrality of its influence in the doctrinal formation of the Latin American military and its consequences in the political sphere (Fitch, 1977; Fox, 2001; Gill, 2004; Shirley, 1997; Wolpin, 1973). (Room, 2021)"

        else:

        break

Convey the meaning of the text: Learning is interactive and embodied. It requires the active participation of both teacher and student to come together to co-create collaborative projects of discovery that allow meaning to unfold and develop between them. This article advances a theory of the intersubjective and embodied nature of cognition and meaning-making, consisting of co-created narrative units. Learning in embodied narrative episodes includes affective, energetic, and intentional components to create interaction patterns that structure knowledge and become memory-retained units. We explore two cases of non-verbal narrative patterns of interaction between a teacher and a child within the practice of the Educational Group, a special pedagogy that is attuned to the affects and interests of children. Analysis of these cases reveals patterns that establish a common rhythm, affect, and body movement between teacher and child, which, when completed, bring shared joy and learning. In this way, we identify an embodied, co-created narrative structure of embodied cognition that is necessary for learning and collaborative thinking.

When a cone of cold hits a target, it deals damage and freezes them. This can lead to unconsciousness or even death if the damage was strong enough.

Sam Altman on the societal impact of AI:
the adaptation to a world deeply integrated with AI tools is probably going to happen pretty quickly; the benefits (and fun!) have too much upside.
...
these tools will help us be more productive (can't wait to spend less time doing email!), healthier (AI medical advisors for people who can’t afford care), smarter (students using [LLM Name] to learn), and more entertained (AI memes lolol).
...
a transition like this is mostly good, and can happen somewhat fast—the transition from the pre-smartphone world to post-smartphone world is a recent example. but it’ll be tempting to go super quickly, which is frightening—society needs time to adapt to something so big.
...
there will be more challenges like bias (we don’t want [LLM Name] to be pro or against any politics by default, but if you want either then it should be for you; working on this now) and people coming away unsettled from talking to a chatbot, even if they know what’s really going on
...
we think showing these tools to the world early, while still somewhat broken, is critical if we are going to have sufficient input and repeated efforts to get it right. the level of individual empowerment coming is wonderful, but not without serious challenges.
...
we also need enough time for our institutions to figure out what to do. regulation will be critical and will take time to figure out; although current-generation AI tools aren’t very scary, i think we are potentially not that far away from potentially scary ones.
...
having time to understand what’s happening, how people want to use these tools, and how society can co-evolve is critical.

Today, I would like to address the issue of artificial intelligence language models and their potential impact on unemployment. As you may already know, AI language models are becoming increasingly sophisticated and have the ability to write and generate large amounts of text. But what does this mean for jobs and the workforce?

Firstly, let's consider the jobs that are at immediate risk of being replaced by AI language models. One of the most obvious examples is content writing. AI language models are already being used to generate news articles, product descriptions, and even social media posts. This means that the need for human content writers may significantly decrease in the coming years, leading to a loss of jobs for those who make a living through writing.

Another job at risk is that of translators. AI language models are rapidly improving their ability to learn and understand different languages, leading to more accurate translations that require little to no human input. This could lead to a decline in the demand for human translators as AI becomes more proficient in this task.

But it's not just these jobs that are at risk. In fact, a study by the McKinsey Global Institute predicts that up to 800 million jobs could be lost

import discord
import requests
import asyncio
from config import url, channel_id, token, params

client = discord.Client(intents=discord.Intents.default())

# List of already sent transactions
sent_transactions = []

# Function to send messages to Discord
async def send_discord_message(message):
    channel = client.get_channel(channel_id)
    await channel send(message)

# Function to check for new transactions
async def check_transactions():
    while True:
        try:
            # Send a request to the whale-alert.io API
            response = requests.get(url, params=params).json()

            # Process response and send messages to Discord
            for transaction in response['transactions']:
                if transaction['id'] not sent_transactions:
                    if transaction['transaction_type'] == 'mint':
                        message = f"🖨️ {transaction['symbol'].upper()} issued in the amount of ${transaction['amount']:,}"
                        await send_discord_message(message)
                    if transaction['transaction_type'] == 'burn':
                        message = f"🔥 Burned {transaction['symbol'].upper()} worth ${transaction['amount']:,}"
                        await send_discord_message(message)
                    sent_transactions.append(transaction['id'])
                    if len(sent_transactions) >= 100:
                        sent_transactions.pop(0)
                    print(sent_transactions)

        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error: {e}")

        # Check for new transactions every 10 seconds
        await asyncio.sleep(10)

# Start the Discord bot and check for new transactions
@client.event
async def on_ready():
    print(f"Bot started: {client.user}")
    await check_transactions()

client.run(token)

She makes predictions based on the best information she has, even if it isn't always reliable or accurate.     """Apply func to L and return the result.

to detect a variety of sensory stimuli. Ask the patient to
close his or her eyes. Start distally and work proximally,
comparing left and right sides. Ask the patient to identify
when and where he or she is touched. Employ a variety of
stimuli, including light touch (use a wisp of gauze or tissue),
pain (use a sharp object such as the broken end of a
sterile tongue depressor), and vibration (place a vibrating
tuning fork over a bony prominence). A thorough examination
tests all major peripheral nerves and dermatomes
(Figure 4-61). Assess tactile localization, proprioception,
graphesthesia (Box 4-14), and point localization.
The monofilament test (Figure 4-62; see Box 4-14) is
commonly used to assess sensory function in the feet of
diabetic patients and in patients receiving neurotoxic
drugs such as vincristine. With the patient seated, inspect
the feet for lesions or ulcers. Then direct the patient to
close his or her eyes and to say yes when the patient feels
the monofilament. Test the pads of the first and fourth
toes and several locations on the plantar surface of the
foot. Touch the skin with the tip of a 10-g monofilament
and apply enough pressure to bend the monofilament;
touch the patient for about 1.5 seconds at each spot.
Observe the patient for abnormal involuntary muscle
movements, resting muscle tone, and strength against
resistance. Muscle strength is evaluated using a plus scale,
with 0 representing no muscle contraction (complete
paralysis) and 5+ representing normal muscle strength
(Table 4-14). Evaluate motor function by assessing muscle
tone during passive flexion and extension (increased
resistance, normal, decreased resistance), abduction and
adduction, and flexion and extension (Box 4-15). A thorough
examination tests all muscle groups in both the
upper and lower extremities."

The "optimization" quadrant contains six service functions:
- Service level management. Manage the quality of IT services by defining and controlling the parameters of the service level agreement between the customer and the supplier
-    Financial management. Definition, control and optimization of IT service costs, which includes calculation and budgeting, analysis of the cost of services and measures to optimize IT costs and assess the need for investments
- Service continuity management. Recovery of IT infrastructure in case of unforeseen situations. Consists of developing a plan to quickly restore business-critical systems
- Capacity management. Control and ensure the required performance of IT resources in accordance with the designated service level SLA
- Accessibility management. Managing the availability of information in terms of the use of available capacities
-    Personnel Management. Recommendations for the selection, retention and motivation of IT staff
The maintenance quadrant consists of the following 8 functions:
- Work planning. Organization of the work process in the most efficient way to meet the requirements of service level agreements
-    System administration. Ensuring the smooth operation of the network infrastructure
- Network administration. It consists in ensuring the security of IP, in determining and controlling the parameters for protecting corporate information and IT services
- Administration of the security system. Consists of IP protection, control and definition of protection parameters of corporate IT services

root = Tk()
root.title("Login")
root.geometry("300x150")
root.resizable(width=FALSE, height=FALSE)
root['background']='#008080'

# User logins and passwords
users = {
    "user1": "password1",
    "user2": "password2",
    "user3": "password3",
    "user4": "password4",
    "user5": "password5"
}

# Function to check login and password
def check_credentials():
    username = username_entry.get()
    password = password_entry.get()
    if username in users and password == users[username]:
        messagebox.showinfo("Logged in", "You have successfully logged in!")



    else:
         messagebox.showerror("Login failed", "Invalid username or password")



# Create labels and input fields for login and password
username_label = tk.Label(root, text="Login:")
username_label.pack()
username_entry = tk.Entry(root)
username_entry.pack()

password_label = tk.Label(root, text="Password:")
password_label.pack()
password_entry = tk.Entry(root, show="*")
password_entry.pack()

# Create login button
login_button = tk.Button(root, text="Login", command=check_credentials)
login_button.pack()


# Run the application
root.mainloop()

Previously typed: In the memoir The Glass Castle, the author Jeannette Walls uses symbolism to convey powerful themes of resilience and determination. Through the use of imagery and metaphors, Walls creates a narrative that speaks to the strength of the human spirit in the face of adversity. From the glass castle to the changing journey of Jeannette, Walls use of symbolism helps to show the difficulties, and the yet uplifting, message of the memoir. Through symbolism, the book serves as a reminder of the power of the human spirit in the face of adversity.

In the Memoir  The Glass Castle, Rex believes that items like toys, or money is unrealistic, and he encourages his children to think about bigger things than just goods. “Years from now, when all the junk they got is broken and long forgotten, you’ll still have your stars” (Walls, 41). This quote is used when Rex is trying to show his children that they should value knowledge more than material items. He also says, “You can’t take things with you, but you can take knowledge and experience with you” (Walls, 91). Rex’s words provide the idea that the things that are truly meaningful are those that can't be taken away.

While the spell is being cast, magical effects begin to occur. Magic begins to gather around the mage, creating a magic cone in front of him. The cone is composed of ice crystals and cold air, and its effect on the target can be devastating.

When the spell is activated, the mage radiates a cone of cold from his hands that spreads in all directions in front of him. The mage controls the direction and distance the cone will spread.

When a cone of cold hits a target, it deals damage and freezes them. This can lead to unconsciousness or even death if the damage was strong enough.

Use the information below to answer the following question: "My house has a large plot of land of 270m2. Because my father refused to transfer the name to my brother, my brother abused him for many days, causing my father to become depressed and After that, my brother was put on trial, the court sentenced him to 4 years and 3 months in prison. However, before committing suicide, my father silently wrote a will to leave the entire land to my brother. , the will was notarized at Notary X's Office, my father completely voluntarily left it to my brother, but because he was afraid that my brother would be addicted to drugs, he would sell the land for money, so he did not tell the family. I lost my family only to find out about this will. After returning from the sentence, will my brother receive the inheritance left by my father? Thank you!"

Civil Code 2015
Article 621. Persons not entitled to inheritance
1. The following people are not entitled to inherit:
a) Persons convicted of intentional acts of infringing upon life or health or of serious mistreatment, torturing estate leavers, seriously infringing upon that person's honor and dignity;
b) The person seriously violates the obligation to nurture the estate leaver;
c) The person is convicted of intentionally infringing upon the life of another heir in order to enjoy part or the whole of the estate to which such heir is entitled;
d) Persons who commit acts of deception, coercion or obstruction of estate leavers in making wills; forging wills, modifying wills, canceling wills, concealing wills in order to enjoy part or all of the estate against the will of the estate leavers.
2. The persons specified in Clause 1 of this Article are still entitled to the estate, if the estate leavers knew their acts, but still allowed them to enjoy the estate according to the will.

Article 630. Lawful wills
1. A lawful will must fully satisfy the following conditions:
a) The testator is lucid and lucid while making the will; not be deceived, threatened or coerced;
b) The content of the will does not violate the prohibition of the law or against social ethics; the form of a will is not contrary to the provisions of the law.
2. Wills of persons aged between full fifteen years and under eighteen years must be made in writing and must be approved by their parents or guardians on the making.
3. The will of a person with physical limitations or of an illiterate person must be made in writing by a witness and notarized or authenticated.
4. A written will without notarization or authentication shall only be considered lawful if all the conditions specified in Clause 1 of this Article are satisfied.
5. An oral will is considered legal if the oral testator shows his/her last will in front of at least two witnesses and immediately after the oral testator shows his/her final will, the witness records copy, co-sign or fingerprint. Within 5 working days from the date the oral testator expresses his/her final will, the will must be certified by a notary public or a competent authority to certify the signature or fingerprints of the witness.

MARRIAGE AND FAMILY LAW 2000 NO. 22/2000/QH10
Article 9. Conditions for marriage
A man and a woman getting married must comply with the following conditions:
1. Men aged twenty years or older, women eighteen years or older;
2. The marriage is decided by a man and a woman voluntarily, neither party may force or deceive the other; no one may coerce or impede;
3. The marriage does not fall into one of the cases where marriage is prohibited specified in Article 10 of this Law.

Beings who deal with runes are called "Runologists", they are eternal seekers and travelers around the world wandering in search of ancient runes, studying ancient writings, architecture and various kinds of artifacts to unravel the way they work and the flow of magic in artifacts. The concept itself arose not by chance, with the rune they associate the enclosed magical energy aimed at a certain effect, the impact on the object itself or on the rest of the objects surrounding the “branded” object. Many powerful artifacts also contain runic seals, which serve as a kind of “protection” for such an object from outsiders, using a complex ornament and meaning, which runologists struggle with on a regular basis. The very use of runes in the everyday perspective became possible almost immediately, however, the complexity of making even the simplest runes caused many "cases" in which novice runologists and others were injured, often leading to death. The current appearance of the runes is the result of a huge amount of work by Runologists to stabilize, copy, change ancient signs, even the meaning of which is almost impossible to explain before a whole series of various experiments on them. In our world - Runes can be used for everything. Their effects are varied, most of the runes are combined with each other due to their aforementioned fabrication.

At the moment, runes can:

1. Be imposed on a specific item in order to achieve a permanent effect from this item.

2. Be imposed on a specific item in order to achieve a one-time effect from the item.

3. Be imposed on a specific entity in order to achieve a one-time or temporary effect from the entity itself.

4. Be applied to a specific surface in order to achieve a one-time or temporary effect from the surface itself.

import tkinter as tk
import datetime
import time
import pygame

root = tk.Tk()
root.title("Hourly Sound Reminder")
root.geometry("200x100")

def play_sound():
    pygame.init()
    pygame.mixer.music.load("C:\\Users\\Aaron\\Downloads\\Music\\mixkit-modern-classic-door-bell-sound-113.wav")
    pygame.mixer.music.play()
    time.sleep(60)  # Play for 60 seconds
    pygame.mixer.music.stop()

def sound_reminder():
    current_time = datetime.datetime.now()
    play_sound()
    root.after(current_time.hour * 3600 * 1000, sound_reminder)

def stop_reminder():
    root.after_cancel(sound_reminder)

start_button = tk.Button(root, text="Start", command=sound_reminder)
stop_button = tk.Button(root, text="Stop", command=stop_reminder)

start_button.pack()
stop_button.pack()

root.mainloop()

Summarize this into two separate sentences: College admissions are competitive, and applicants want to make sure they showcase their best, “perfect” selves on their application. The high pressure to stand out and the lack of clarity on what an institution is looking for in their admits contributes to the applicants’ stress regarding the application. As important as college admissions may seem, students must be reassured of their anxieties.

Applications often ask questions that do not fit some students’ situations, and provide no reasoning behind why certain questions must be answered. The format of college applications, between their questions, essay(s) and required documents,  add to the challenge applicants face with representing their “wholes” selves. Applicants benefit from being encouraged to tell their truth and share their personal experiences, in a safe space where they can showcase their capabilities, to overcome adversity. 

The process of filling out a college application is an emotional one. Applicants across the board oscillate between excitement, anxiety and nervousness that are prompted by the pressure to stand out in a competitive environment, getting the financial support they need and their current life situation.  While high school students may already be burnt out by an exhausting curriculum at school, adult learners struggle with finding the motivation to take the actions they need to apply to college.

At just USD $29.90 per month, users will get access to the entire MOOAR product. This cost is then made-up by the 0% service fees on MOOAR, which usually turns into substantial savings. For instance, if you trade a Bored Ape Yacht Club (BAYC) NFT for 60 ETH at a marketplace that charges 2% platform fees, that fee would cover 5 years of MOOAR membership from that transaction alone.

Similar to how warehouse clubs like Costco operate, the recurring fee will turn MOOAR into a self-sufficient entity. By targeting to cover all necessary development and maintenance costs with this fee, this allows us to offer a 0% service fee, which most traditional NFT marketplaces generate the bulk of their revenue from.

```#!/bin/bash

# Get the current hour
HOUR=$(date +%H)

# Check if the current hour is 0 (midnight)
if [ "$HOUR" -eq 0 ]; then
    # If it is, set the number of downloads to 1
    DOWNLOADS=1
else
    # Otherwise, set the number of downloads to the current hour
    DOWNLOADS="$HOUR"
fi

# Create a new directory for the downloaded files

if [ "$1" = "download" ]; then

    DIRNAME="kumpulan_$(($(ls -d kumpulan_* | wc -l) + 1))"
    mkdir "$DIRNAME"
    # Download the files
    for ((i = 1; i <= DOWNLOADS; i++)); do
        FILENAME="perjalanan_$i"
        wget "https://id.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soekarno#/media/Berkas:Presiden_Sukarno.jpg" -O "$DIRNAME/$FILENAME"
    done

elif [ "$1" = "zip" ]; then

    DEVIL_ZIP_COUNT=$(ls -l | grep -c "devil_[0-9]*\.zip")
    NEXT_DEVIL_ZIP_NAME="devil_$((DEVIL_ZIP_COUNT + 1)).zip"
    # Find all directories with names starting with "kumpulan_"
    DIRECTORIES=$(find . -type d -name "kumpulan_*")
    # Zip all directories and move the zip file to the backup directory
    zip -r $NEXT_DEVIL_ZIP_NAME $DIRECTORIES
else
    echo "Usage: $0 [download|zip]"
    exit 1
fi```   Refactor kode ini, dan buatlah susunannya berbeda dari sebelumnya

A string s1 is an anagram of string s2 if its letters can be rearranged to form s2. For example, "listen" is an anagram of "silent", and "admirer" is an anagram of "married". For this question, a word is considered to be an anagram of itself.

Consider this code:

def is_anagram(s1: str, s2: str) -> bool:
    """Return True if and only if s1 is an anagram of s2.

> is_anagram("silent", "listen")
>     True
>     >>> is_anagram("bear", "breach")
>     False
>     """
> 
> Select the algorithm(s) that can be used to implement is_anagram.
> 
> 
> 
> Create a list of the characters in s1. (The length of this list will be len(s1).)
> Create a list of the characters in s2. (The length of this list will be len(s2).)
> For each item in the list of characters from s1, remove one occurrence of that item from the list of characters from s2 (if it exists).
> If the list of characters from s2 becomes empty, s1 is an anagram of s2.
> 
> Create a dictionary d1 in which each key is a letter from s1 and each value is the number of occurrences of that letter in s1.
> Create a dictionary d2 in which each key is a letter from s2 and each value is the number of occurrences of that letter in s2.
> If d1 == d2, then s1 is an anagram of s2.
> For each letter in s1, count the number of occurrences of the letter in s1 and count the number of occurrences of the letter in s2. If each letter in s1 occurs the same number of times in s1 and s2, then s1 is an anagram of s2.
> For each letter in s2, count the number of occurrences of the letter in s1 and count the number of occurrences of the letter in s2. If each letter in s2 occurs the same number of times in s1 and s2, then s1 is an anagram of s2.
> 
> Create a list L1 of the characters in s1. (The length of this list will be len(s1).)
> Create a list L2 of the characters in s2. (The length of this list will be len(s2).)
> Sort both lists.
> If L1 == L2, s1 is an anagram of s2.

The following text should be described in midjourney: The sky of the earth was suddenly enveloped in darkness, as if night had fallen. However, this is not an ordinary night, but the arrival of the Trisolaran fleet. Their ships are like huge flying saucers, lined up, covering the sun's brilliance. People on the earth looked up at this sudden catastrophe in horror and were at a loss. The trisolaran fleet is constantly marching towards the earth, and their hulls glow with faint blue light, as if they are ghosts from the depths of the universe. As the distance draws closer, people on Earth gradually see their true colors: huge three-body warships, their hulls are like spiders, covered with sharp tentacles, as if they are ready to tear apart everything that stands in front of them at any time. enemy. The earth's defense forces quickly assembled, and all countries united to resist foreign enemies. Fighter planes roared in the air, tanks roared forward on the ground, and countless brave soldiers took up arms and swore to defend their homeland. However, the enemy they faced was simply too powerful. The weapons of the Trisolaran battleship can almost ignore the existing defense methods of the earth, and their attacks are like the scythe of death, easily harvesting the lives of the people on earth. The battle was extremely fierce, and the cities on the earth were reduced to ruins under the attack of the Trisolaran warship. A huge fireball exploded in the sky, shining with terrifying light. On the ground, countless soldiers and civilians struggled to survive amid the gunfire and ruins, but their efforts seemed to be in vain. Under the iron hooves of the Trisolarans, the civilization of the earth seems to be in jeopardy. However, in this dark hour, Earthlings have not given up hope. They resisted tenaciously, looking for opportunities to counterattack. Scientists worked day and night to study the technology of the Trisolarans, trying to find their weaknesses. The soldiers on the front line built a solid line of defense with flesh and blood, defending the last inch of land on the earth to the death. In this arduous battle, the people on earth gradually found the weakness of the Trisolarans and successfully developed weapons that could fight them. At the critical moment when the earth was about to be conquered, the people on earth launched a counterattack that shook the world and wept ghosts and gods. In this battle, the people on Earth paid a heavy price, but finally succeeded in repelling the invasion of the Trisolarans. This war has cost the people on Earth a huge price, but it has also made them realize the power of unity and bravery. On the way to restore their homeland, people on earth will work together to rebuild a better future. And those heroes who sacrificed their lives to defend their homeland will remain in the hearts of people on earth forever and become an indelible spiritual pillar.

Continue the text, describe how the storm was a reflection of Spectra

“Summoned, my prince?” - kneeling before the throne, the fighter of Pyrus asked, not allowing himself to raise his eyes above the snow-white trousers of the ruler, thereby interrupting the tea. However, this did not interfere with the bad weather raging outside the wide window. Each clap of thunder followed one after another, as if tearing apart dark clouds, outlining zigzags of lightning in them. And so it had been for several hours since the colonization operation began to take an irreversible turn. Disobedience, freedom are the primary irritants of any absolutist. “That thunder,” the prince’s voice broke the silence, mingled with a note of self-control. “I think he likes to sound in our ears. And get on your nerves. Taking a sip from the cup, he allowed himself a short pause, then to continue coldly, distantly: - And what is this for? Where does this malice come from? “I dare to suggest that this is a consequence of the perturbation of the core of New Vestroia. When it will stop is hard to say. But now it is gaining strength,” the blond reported without taking his eyes off the floor. The face of the royal was calm, even a little tired. Many would think that their sovereign is in melancholy. Clicking his tongue, the prince turned his gaze to the wide window, almost to the floor. After a while, he shook his head. “Let you get up and go to the window so that the forecasts are more accurate, Spectra, because the clarity of your thoughts has not hurt anyone yet,” he said. With these words, his fingers played with the royal curls. The prince followed his whim closely, and mischievous sparks flickered in his amethyst eyes. However, they were cold. Approaching the window opening, Spectra looked around the open space in front of him. For a second, he thought he looked into his own reflection.

summarise accurately in as few words as possible , encapsulate all of this information in bullet points. Everything covered but in the least amount of words possible: A contract may give rise to a duty to act, which may then lead to criminal liability if the defendant fails to meet his contractual obligations, and this leads to a fatality or serious harm being caused to a person. This type of duty usually arises in the context of an employment contract where the employee has failed to do what he is contractually obliged to do, and it may be owed to people who are not necessarily party to the contract, but who are likely to be injured by a failure to perform the contract (as in the example at 2.4.2.3 involving the electrician who leaves cabling exposed causing a fatality). For instance, if a lifeguard notices a person drowning and does not attempt to save them, or if she fails to notice a person drowning in the swimming pool because she has become distracted by checking her mobile phone for messages, she could be held criminally liable for the death of the victim if she is deemed to have failed to perform her contractual duty.

Continue: what are the main personality neoplasms that arise in the course of human development from birth to 17 years, according to L.I. Bozovic?
Lidia Ilyinichna Bozhovich was a Russian psychologist who studied the personal development of a person throughout his life. She identified several key stages of personality development from birth to 17 years, at each of which the main personality neoplasms arise:

1. Early childhood (0-3 years): At this stage, the main neoplasms are the formation of emotional ties with the mother and other close people, the development of self-awareness and the beginning of awareness of oneself as a separate person.

2. Preschool age (3-6 years): At this stage, children begin to master social norms and rules, an idea of ​​themselves in social roles is formed, play activities develop in which children learn to interact with others and take on different roles.

3. Junior school age (7-10 years): During this period, adaptation to school life occurs, the formation of educational motivation and interests. The main new formations are the development of learning skills, the ability to work in a team, the formation of self-esteem and the beginning of the development of moral and ethical principles.

4. Adolescence (11-14 years old): At this stage, neoplasms arise associated with the formation of self-identity, awareness of one's individuality and group membership. Adolescents are actively looking for their place in the social environment, develop their interests and beliefs, form their own style of communication and behavior.

5. Adolescence (15-17 years): At this stage, the main neoplasms are the development of independence, the ability to take responsibility for one's actions and make life decisions. Boys and girls
